title: Take-Two Schedules August 7 Earnings Call, Sparking GTA 6 Trailer 3 Speculation
summary: Take-Two Interactive announced its fiscal Q1 2027 earnings call for August 7 at 8 AM Eastern, an unusually early Friday time slot before markets open. Fans believe the timing change suggests Rockstar may release the long-awaited third GTA 6 trailer around this date to boost investor confidence and capture first pre-order sales data from the June 25 launch window.

title: Rockstar UK Developers Seek Union Recognition Before GTA 6 Launch
summary: Rockstar Games employees across UK studios filed for union recognition on June 30, backed by the IWGB, seeking protections on crunch, pay transparency, and flexible working. The newly formed Rockstar Game Workers Union warned that strike action remains possible if the company refuses voluntary recognition, potentially disrupting the game's final development months before its November 19 release.

title: GTA 6 Physical Disc Confusion Clarified: No Real Discs Ever Planned
summary: An email from Rockstar customer support caused confusion by suggesting physical discs would arrive months after launch. The Hollywood Reporter confirmed with sources that GTA 6 will remain digital-only with code-in-box retail versions, clarifying that the email's reference to physical copies meant the boxed codes available in stores following pre-order announcements, not actual game discs.

title: Rockstar Website Update Fuels Third Trailer Speculation
summary: Rockstar Games is currently updating its website and has removed the pre-order banner, a pattern that has historically preceded major announcements. While website updates have sometimes related to other games in the past, fans are speculating this could signal the imminent arrival of the mythical third GTA 6 trailer, which would be the first major reveal since the second trailer dropped in May 2025.

title: Developer Strike Threat Looms Over GTA 6 Final Crunch Period
summary: Rockstar developers are preparing potential strike action during the critical crunch period leading up to GTA 6's November 19 launch. The unionization push follows the firing of 31 employees in October 2025, with workers now demanding protections around overtime practices, job security, and workplace conditions across the company's Edinburgh, Dundee, Lincoln, Leeds, and London studios.
